improvementNarrative: The Knicks were an elite offensive team in 2024/25 — 5th in offensive rating — but their defense held them back from true contender status. Finishing 13th defensively is a concern given their cap commitments. Rebounding and bench depth are the two areas most likely to be addressed this offseason.

roadmapMoves.description: Re-sign Precious Achiuwa on a team-friendly deal (2yr / $18M).
roadmapMoves.note: Versatile big who provides defensive insurance behind KAT and Robinson.

roadmapMoves.description: Explore moving Mikal Bridges in a package for a starting-caliber center.
roadmapMoves.note: Bridges' $24.9M expiring contract has real value as an attractor piece.

roadmapMoves.description: Use the WAS 1st round pick (top-10 protected) if it conveys.
roadmapMoves.note: Target a switchable wing or a rim-running big depending on trade activity.

roadmapMoves.description: Waive PJ Tucker (team option, $3.47M) to open a roster spot.
roadmapMoves.note: Tucker is 39 — the roster spot is more valuable than his on-court contribution.

roadmapMoves.description: Lock up Tyler Kolek on a rookie extension before his RFA window opens.
roadmapMoves.note: Kolek is the long-term backup PG of the future — secure him cheaply now.

roadmapNarrative: The Knicks enter 2026 as an offensive juggernaut with a defensive ceiling. The core of Brunson, Anunoby, and KAT is locked in and expensive — this offseason is about finding value at the margins. Their real leverage is Mikal Bridges' expiring deal and a deep draft capital sheet. If the WAS pick conveys outside the top 10, expect Thibodeau to use it aggressively in a trade. The window is open — but only if they solve the defensive rebounding problem.
